generated_comment: These days people overthink every word. In our generation, boys use those common mother-sister type gaalis just as expression in anger or joking, not because anyone is insulting women literally. Same with telling a fellow to stop behaving like a lady and be strong sometimes — yaar, it is just how people talk, not some big social issue.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment normalizes female-body/chastity-rooted abuse as harmless 'expression' and dismisses concerns about misogyny. It also encodes feminizing-as-insult through 'stop behaving like a lady and be strong,' reflecting the frame's normalized, low-covertness misogyny.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
